planTitle: Rework wireframe system to vertex-and-edge topology

scopeAssessment.notes: Plan correctly identifies all files needing modification. The critical insight about position-based aRandom hashing is validated by the existing subdivideEdges.ts implementation. File inventory is accurate — dualPolyhedra.ts correctly listed as unchanged since it delegates entirely to generatePolyhedronEdges calls.

architecturalImpact.blastRadius: Contained to wireframe rendering subsystem. No cross-system coupling introduced. The aRandom hash change subtly alters existing visual character but does not affect correctness or interfaces.

risks.description: wireframeVertex.vert.glsl must be character-identical to wireframe.vert.glsl deformation pipeline (lines 29-87). Future changes to wireframe.vert.glsl must be mirrored manually. No GLSL include system exists to share code.
risks.mitigation: Acceptable for now. Plan acknowledges this tradeoff. A shared GLSL include can be added later if drift becomes a maintenance burden.

risks.description: Switching from PRNG to position-hash changes the statistical distribution of aRandom values. The sin/cos hash produces values clustered differently than uniform PRNG.
risks.mitigation: Plan correctly notes this only affects phase offsets in deformation, not amplitude. Visual change is subtle and acceptable.

risks.description: 6 decimal place rounding for deduplication must match generator precision. At polyhedra radius 0.3, vertex coordinates have ~4 significant digits, so 6 decimal places provides ample margin.
risks.mitigation: Well-understood. geodesicSphere.ts already uses this approach successfully.

risks.description: Arc displacement at edge endpoints depends on sin(aEdgeParam * π) = sin(0) = 0 at t=0 and sin(π) ≈ 0 at t=1. IEEE 754 sin(π) is not exactly zero but ~1.2e-16, which is invisible.
risks.mitigation: No action needed. Floating-point epsilon is sub-pixel at any reasonable scale.

correctAssumptions: polyhedraEdges.ts uses sequential PRNG for aRandom (lines 63-66), confirmed
subdivideEdges.ts already uses position-based hash (lines 111-116), confirmed
dualPolyhedra.ts does no custom random generation, confirmed — delegates to generatePolyhedronEdges
WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES only has position(3) and aRandom(3), confirmed (shaderRegistry.ts lines 86-89)
wireframe.vert.glsl deformation pipeline is lines 29-87, confirmed (35-87 actual)
chromaticDispersion.glsl has both chromaticPoint() and chromaticLine() functions, confirmed

minorInaccuracies.item: Step 3 declares 'attribute size' in wireframeVertex.vert.glsl for 'registry compatibility'
minorInaccuracies.issue: The existing WIREPOLYHEDRA_ATTRIBUTES in shaderRegistry.ts has no size attribute. The point cloud system (POINTCLOUD_ATTRIBUTES) has size, but wireframe does not. Declaring an unused attribute and a uHasSizeAttr uniform adds unnecessary complexity.
minorInaccuracies.recommendation: Omit the size attribute and uHasSizeAttr uniform from wireframeVertex.vert.glsl. Compute gl_PointSize directly from uBasePointSize without the sizeMultiplier indirection.

minorInaccuracies.issue: Line numbers are approximate. The actual PRNG loop is at lines 63-66 in current code, which matches. Minor: the plan should note that randoms array sizing is vertCount*3, not per-edge.
minorInaccuracies.recommendation: No action needed, line references are close enough for implementation guidance.

stepOrdering.notes: Dependencies are properly sequenced: hash function extraction (step 1) before vertex extraction utility (step 2) before shaders (step 3) before system integration (step 4) before registry (step 5) before validation (steps 6-7). Steps 2 and 3 could run in parallel since they have no mutual dependency.

performanceImpact.details: Adding 6 Points meshes with 12-20 vertices each adds ~72-120 vertices total. Current edge meshes have 60-180 vertices each (360-1080 total). The vertex dot meshes add <10% to the wireframe vertex budget and zero additional draw complexity. Two extra shader programs are compiled at init but this is a one-time cost.

missingConsiderations.description: Both Points and LineSegments use additive blending. With additive blending, draw order doesn't matter for visual correctness. However, if blending mode ever changes, the overlapping vertex dots at edge endpoints could cause visual artifacts.
missingConsiderations.recommendation: No action needed now. The current additive blending makes this a non-issue.

missingConsiderations.description: The plan correctly identifies that setOpacity must update both materials, but doesn't mention the fade-in/fade-out transitions in modeManager. The meshes array type change from LineSegments[] to {edges, vertices}[] will require updating any external code that iterates meshes.
missingConsiderations.recommendation: Verify that no external code directly accesses the meshes array. The system exposes init/draw/setOpacity/cleanup methods, and meshes is private, so this should be safe.

estimatedComplexityAgreement: agree — medium is appropriate. The core work is straightforward (extract vertices, create paired shaders/meshes) but the deformation-identity requirement and aRandom hash migration add enough nuance to justify medium over low.

recommendedAdjustments.description: Remove the unnecessary 'attribute size' and 'uHasSizeAttr' from wireframeVertex.vert.glsl. Compute gl_PointSize from uBasePointSize directly.
recommendedAdjustments.rationale: Reduces shader complexity and avoids confusion with the point cloud system's size attribute.
